text:
I don't care, this is the greatest thing I've ever seen at the#Olympics#Breakingpic.twitter.com/BTf1cket3g

What was Rachel Gunn even doing at the OlympicsShe got cooked lolhttps://t.co/SYSkNlFgCu

Breakdancing competitions were held for the first time at the Olympics in Paris.And the main hero of this 'sport- 36-year-old Rachel Gunn from the Australian team.She didn't even score a point from any of the judges. She says my dance moves were original.Do watch the video!pic.twitter.com/t3PivHGcR9

Dr Rachel “Raygun” Gunn, PhD in breakdancing & dance culture, Macquarie University lecturer:“All my moves are original, creativity is really important to me. I go out there and I show my artistry. I do my thing, and it represents art.”Yeah, art is so subjective… 🦘 🏊‍♀️ 🌪️ 💃https://t.co/ENsR2EalKH

What people don’t get is that 1/2 the event is break, but the other 1/2 is a rap battle where Raygun had a significant lyrical advantage over her competitors. For ex here’s her round 1 verse:Well my first name’s RachelMy last name’s GunnI’m coming out hereTo have some fun!https://t.co/Lre78OVhqk

I speak for everyone when I say breakdancing should not be in the Olympicspic.twitter.com/WAc2Nj09P9

GENERAL

Srishti B Dutta

title: 'What was she even doing at the Olympics': The Internet can't get over lecturer Rachel Gunn's 'Breaking' performance—here's why
description: 36-year-old Rachel Gunn, an Aussie breakdancer with a PhD in cultural studies, made headlines at the Paris 2024 Olympics for her less-than-stellar performance at Breaking which made its debut this year. And the internet just can't seem to get over her performance.
